@context.description: A list of JSON-LD context URIs that define the semantic meaning of properties within the credential.

id.description: A unique identifier (URI) assigned to this verifiable credential.

issuer.description: The organisation that is the issuer of this VC. Note that the "id" property MUST be a W3C DID.  Other identifiers such as tax registration numbers can be listed in the "otherIdentifiers" property.

validFrom.description: The date and time from which the credential is valid.

validUntil.description: The expiry date (if applicable) of this verifiable credential.

name.description: Name of this verifiable credential instance (eg the title of a digital product passport, facility record, lifecycle event, or conformity credential)

credentialStatus.description: A W3C VCDM2.0 compliant object containing credential status information.

renderMethod.description: Human rendering information for this credential. An array of render methods (eg RenderTemplate2024) that may be used to display the credential.

credentialSubject.description: The registered identity from the authoritative register that is the subject of this credential

issuingSoftware.description: Optional metadata identifying the software product (and its vendor) that issued this credential. Useful for vendor traceability and conformity testing. Issuers MAY omit this property.

description: The Digital Identity Anchor (DIA) is a very simple credential that is issued by a trusted authority and asserts an equivalence between a member identity as known to the authority (eg a VAT number) and one or more decentralised identifiers (DIDs) held by the member.

id.description: The W3C DID of the issuer - should be a did:web or did:webvh

name.description: The name of the issuer person or organisation

id.description: Globally unique identifier of this party. Typically represented as a URI identifierScheme/Identifier URI

name.description: Legal registered name of this party.

registeredId.description: The registration number (alphanumeric) of the Party within the register. Unique within the register.

issuerAlsoKnownAs.description: An optional list of other registered identifiers for this credential issuer

CredentialIssuer.description: The issuer party (person or organisation) of a verifiable credential.

type.description: The type of status list - must be set to "The type property MUST be BitstringStatusListEntry."

id.description: optional identifier of this status list entry.

statusPurpose.description: Status purpose drawn from a standard list but extensible as per w3c bitstring status list specification.

statusListIndex.description: The statusListIndex property MUST be an arbitrary size integer greater than or equal to 0, expressed as a string in base 10. The value identifies the position of the status of the verifiable credential.

statusListCredential.description: The statusListCredential property MUST be a URL to a verifiable credential. When the URL is dereferenced, the resulting verifiable credential MUST have type property that includes the BitstringStatusListCredential value.

BitstringStatusListEntry.description: A privacy-preserving, space-efficient, and high-performance mechanism for publishing status information such as suspension or revocation of Verifiable Credentials through use of bitstrings.  See https://www.w3.org/TR/vc-bitstring-status-list/ for full details.

name.description: Human facing display name for selection

mediaQuery.description: Media query as defined in https://www.w3.org/TR/mediaqueries-4/

template.description: An inline template field for use cases where remote retrieval of a render method is suboptimal

mediaType.description: media type of the rendered output (eg text/html)

digestMultibase.description: Used for resource integrity and/or validation of the inline `template`

RenderTemplate2024.description: A single template format focused render method where the content/media type decision becomes secondary (and is expressed separately).See https://github.com/w3c-ccg/vc-render-method/issues/9

id.description: A resolvable identifier for the specific version of the software product that issued this credential.

name.description: The name of the software product that issued this credential.

version.description: The version of the software product that issued this credential.

id.description: The decentralised identifier (DID) or other resolvable identifier of the software vendor.

name.description: The name of the software vendor.

vendor.description: The vendor of the software product that issued this credential.

IssuingSoftware.description: Optional metadata identifying the software product (and its vendor) that issued the parent credential. When present, all listed sub-properties MUST be populated; when absent, the credential is still valid (verifiers MUST treat the property as optional).

id.description: The DID that is controlled by the registered member and is linked to the registeredID through this Identity Anchor credential

registeredName.description: The registered name of the entity within the identifier scheme.  Examples: product - EV battery 300Ah, Party - Sample Company Pty Ltd,  Facility - Green Acres battery factory

registeredId.description: The registration number (alphanumeric) of the entity within the register. Unique within the register.

registeredDate.description: The date on which this identity was first registered with the registrar.

publicInformation.description: A link to further information about the registered entity on the authoritative registrar site.

idScheme.description: The identifier scheme operated by the registrar

id.description: Globally unique identifier of this party. Typically represented as a URI identifierScheme/Identifier URI

name.description: Legal registered name of this party.

registrar.description: The registrar party that operates the register.

registerType.description: The thematic purpose of the register - organisations, facilities, products, trademarks, etc

registrationScope.description: List of URIs that represent the roles or scopes of membership. For example ["https://abr.business.gov.au/Help/EntityTypeDescription?Id=19"]

RegisteredIdentity.description: The identity anchor is a mapping between a registry member identity and one or more decentralised identifiers owned by the member. It may also list a set of membership scopes.

id.description: The URI of this identifier scheme

name.description: The name of the identifier scheme.

IdentifierScheme.description: An identifier registration scheme for products, facilities, or organisations. Typically operated by a state, national or global authority.
